summary: PR 750 made a one-line GitHub Actions workflow fix in `.github/workflows/editorconfig.yml`, changing the `actions/checkout@v3` ref used by the EditorConfig check under `pull_request_target` from `${{github.ref}}` to `refs/pull/${{github.event.pull_request.number}}/merge`. The change matters because `pull_request_target` defaults to checking out the base branch, so the workflow needs an explicit pull-request merge ref to validate the PR content rather than only the base branch. No discussion or review-comment rationale was present in the slice beyond the title, diff, and inline workflow comment.
what_changed: Updated the checkout ref in `.github/workflows/editorconfig.yml` for the EditorConfig workflow.
Kept the existing workflow comment noting that `pull_request_target` checks out the base branch by default.
smart_moves: Used the explicit GitHub pull request merge ref format so the workflow can check the PR merge result instead of relying on the default base-branch checkout behavior of `pull_request_target`.
Limited the fix to a minimal one-line workflow change, reducing risk for CI infrastructure.
decomp_lessons: CI checks that validate formatting or repository policy must be run against the actual PR content; for `pull_request_target`, explicitly set the checkout ref when the default base branch is not desired.
Small workflow ref changes can affect whether contributor changes are actually tested, even when the job appears to run successfully.
follow_up_queries: Find related PRs that changed `.github/workflows/editorconfig.yml` or `pull_request_target` checkout behavior.
Check whether PRs around this date introduced `${{github.ref}}` in the EditorConfig workflow and why it was reverted.
